response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer (~13 years) with strong fundamentals in testing, distributed systems, and high-throughput low-latency services. However, this role centers on hands-on C++/STL development, and the candidate's C++ exposure is minimal and dated (a ~6-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a university thesis project). The bulk of their career is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is also no evidence of the required industrial cybersecurity background (SDL, IEC 62443) or MQTT/industrial protocols. Strong adjacent skills, but a poor fit for a C++-first, secure-industrial-automation role.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance, low-latency, concurrent systems work (millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic)
Senior-level breadth across backend, embedded, and event-driven platforms with strong TDD/agile practices
Experience building and operating distributed, message/event-driven systems with full CI/CD and DevOps
concerns: C++/STL experience is minimal and over a decade old; core role requirement not met
No evidence of secure development lifecycle, IEC 62443, or industrial cybersecurity expertise
No exposure to MQTT, OPC UA, or industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0)
